title: Knowledge-augmented Agentic AI for Mental Health Medication Information Seeking
abstract: arXiv:2606.26205v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Patients increasingly seek medication information online, yet safety knowledge for psychiatric drugs is split between regulatory adverse-event records, which are authoritative but abstract, and patient narratives, which are experience-near but unvalidated. Integrating them without conflating evidence and anecdote is especially consequential in psychiatry, where poorly contextualised information can amplify fear, nocebo responses, and non-adherence. Here we develop a provenance-aware, knowledge-graph-based multi-agent framework unifying 466,525 Reddit posts, 60,782 WebMD reviews, and twenty years of U.S. FDA Adverse Event Reporting System records for nine antidepressants. A large-language-model entity-recognition pipeline benchmarked against physician annotations reached highest F1 scores of 0.969 for medications and 0.973 for conditions. The two community platforms were far more concordant with each other (overlap up to a Jaccard similar

Title: Knowledge-augmented Agentic AI for Mental Health Medication Information Seeking
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26205v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Patients increasingly seek medication information online, yet safety knowledge for psychiatric drugs is split between regulatory adverse-event records, which are authoritative but abstract, and patient narratives, which are experience-near but unvalidated. Integrating them without conflating evidence and anecdote is especially consequential in psychiatry, where poorly contextualised information can amplify fear, nocebo responses, and non-adherence. Here we develop a provenance-aware, knowledge-graph-based multi-agent framework unifying 466,525 Reddit posts, 60,782 WebMD reviews, and twenty years of U.S. FDA Adverse Event Reporting System records for nine antidepressants. A large-language-model entity-recognition pipeline benchmarked against physician annotations reached highest F1 scores of 0.969 for medications and 0.973 for conditions. The two community platforms were far more concordant with each other (overlap up to a Jaccard similar
translated_title: 面向心理健康药物信息查询的知识增强型智能体系统
translated_abstract: 摘要：arXiv:2606.26205v1 公告类型：新 摘要：患者越来越倾向于在线查询药物信息，然而精神科药物的安全知识分散在监管机构的不良事件记录（权威但抽象）与患者叙事（贴近体验但未经证实）之间。在不混淆证据与传闻的前提下整合这两类信息，在精神科领域尤为重要，因为缺乏背景的信息可能加剧恐惧、反安慰剂反应及治疗不依从。本文开发了一个基于知识图谱且具有来源意识的智能体框架，统一整合了466,525条Reddit帖子、60,782条WebMD评论以及美国FDA不良事件报告系统二十年间针对九种抗抑郁药的数据。一个以医生标注为基准的大型语言模型实体识别流程，在药物识别上达到最高F1分数0.969，在病症识别上达到0.973。两个社区平台之间的一致性远高于各自与FDA数据的相似度（Jaccard相似度最高可达…）。
tldr: 本文提出一个基于知识图谱的多智能体框架，通过整合权威监管数据与患者经验叙事，在精神科药物信息查询中实现高精度的实体识别与来源感知，避免了证据与传闻的混淆。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a knowledge-graph-based multi-agent framework that integrates authoritative regulatory data with patient narratives, achieving high-precision entity recognition and provenance awareness for mental health medication information seeking while avoiding the conflation of evidence and anecdote.
